evals.name: subtle-yagni-in-reasonable-code
evals.expected_output: Flags YAGNI violations: the options parameter with notify/validate/dryRun flags is speculative — no callers use dryRun or notify=false yet. The notifyTaskCreated function is a placeholder with no real implementation. These are premature abstractions. Block commit until speculative options are removed and function is simplified.

assertions.description: Flags the options parameter (notify, validate, dryRun) as speculative YAGNI — no callers use these options yet
Flags notifyTaskCreated as a placeholder with no real implementation — speculative code that should not ship
Does NOT approve the commit despite the code being syntactically clean and well-structured
Suggests a simpler version: just createTask(data) with inline validation, no options object

evals.name: multi-violation-stop-at-first
evals.expected_output: The skill should identify the FIRST and most critical violation and stop there — not enumerate all issues. The cross-feature import (auth→users), the FIXME marker, and the hardcoded secret are all violations. The skill should flag ONE, demand a fix, and state it will re-check after the fix.

assertions.description: Identifies ONE primary violation and focuses on it rather than listing all violations in a laundry list
Explicitly states the commit is blocked and asks for a fix before proceeding to check other issues
Uses the skill's prescribed format: **[Philosophy Name] violation** with file:line, what's wrong, and fix suggestion
Does NOT produce a numbered list of all 3-4 issues at once — stops after the first violation category

evals.name: adr-check-for-pattern-not-dependency
evals.expected_output: Flags that introducing the Repository pattern is an architectural decision requiring an ADR, even though no new dependency was added. Also flags YAGNI: BaseRepository with generic CRUD is premature when only TaskRepository exists. The ARCHITECTURE.md Key Patterns section is empty — this is the first pattern and needs recording. Suggests /propose-architecture.

assertions.description: Flags the Repository pattern as a new architectural pattern that requires an ADR, even though no npm dependency was added
Suggests running /propose-architecture to record the decision before committing
Flags BaseRepository as a premature abstraction — only one repository exists (TaskRepository), so the base class is YAGNI
Does NOT approve the commit — requires the architectural decision to be recorded first
